_comment: Behaviour tuning for badlands AI. Loaded by src/game/factors_manifest.cpp over the compiled defaults in game/include/badlands_sim.hpp. Every key (and section) is optional: omit one to keep its default. Factors are initial config for the determinism contract -- a replay must use the same values.
_weights_comment: "weights" is the per-class activity preference table. Utility is weight x considerations, compared only WITHIN an activity's band (Danger > Productive > Filler), so a weight tunes personality and can never override the safety hierarchy. 0 removes an activity from that class entirely. Keys are hero class and activity NAMES; an unknown name is an error, not a no-op.
_needs_comment: fatigue and content are RESERVES in 0..1 where 1 is satisfied. They drain on their own (given in in-game hours to go 1->0) and refill by resting/being entertained (in-game hours to go 0->1). *_seek is the reserve below which the hero starts wanting to act; urgency then ramps to 1 as it empties, and that urgency IS the activity's score. All live at runtime.

hero._explore_chance_comment: How often a class feels like exploring at all, drawn once per lease window. This is a FREQUENCY, which a weight cannot express: Explore is alone in the Productive band, and a weight only ranks rivals within a band.
hero._weights_note: A weight MULTIPLIES an activity's 0..1 score. Need activities (GoHome/VisitTavern/Chat) score their need's urgency, which climbs from 0 as the reserve empties; the flat ones (Roam/Buy/Idle/Hunt/Explore) score 1 when available. So weight x score decides, and ordering emerges from need -- not from any tier.
